title: Haven — Summer 2025
excerpt: A look at the Delray Beach market this summer, the latest on Casa Avenida's progress, and the lifestyle awaiting residents just one block from Atlantic Avenue.

body.text: The Market

South Florida's luxury real estate market continues to demonstrate its remarkable resilience. In Delray Beach specifically, demand for boutique product — eight units or fewer, walkable to Atlantic Avenue — remains far outpacing supply. Casa Avenida was conceived precisely to answer this demand: eight curated townhomes where scarcity, craft, and location converge.

Buyers who came to Delray Beach during the 2020–2022 migration wave are now graduating: they know the market, they know what they want, and they are unwilling to compromise on quality or address. Casa Avenida offers both.
On Site

Site preparation is complete. Foundation work is now underway. The 8-residence footprint at 102 SE 5th Ave is taking shape, and the team from RWB-Linares Architecture is on site weekly to ensure that every measurement reflects the precision of the design intent.
Framing is anticipated to begin in Q4 2025, with interior fit-out — where the Brisa and Noir finish selections come to life — targeted for Q3 2026. Completion remains on schedule for Q2 2027.
The Lifestyle

One block from Atlantic Avenue, Casa Avenida residents will step into one of South Florida's most celebrated walkable neighborhoods. Award-winning restaurants, boutique fitness studios, art galleries, and Delray's famous Blue Wave beach are all within five minutes on foot. It is, in the truest sense, a life without a car — for those who choose it.

title: Haven — Fall 2025
excerpt: As South Florida's peak season approaches, we explore what makes Delray Beach the most sought-after address in Palm Beach County, and share updates from the Casa Avenida site.

body.text: Peak Season Arrives

October marks the start of South Florida's real estate season in earnest. Buyers who spent the summer elsewhere return with renewed clarity about what they want — and increasingly, what they want is Delray Beach. The city has quietly become the most in-demand address in Palm Beach County, prized for its walkability, cultural calendar, and the quality of its dining and beach scene.

For boutique luxury product like Casa Avenida, this moment is ideal. We are entering the season with the project well advanced, finish selections resolved, and a clear construction timeline. Buyers at this stage are purchasing with confidence.
Site Update: Framing Begins

Framing is now underway at 102 SE 5th Ave. The four-level structure is rising, and the vertical scale of the residences — generous floor-to-ceiling heights at every level — is already apparent. It is gratifying to see the architectural ambition of RWB-Linares taking physical form.
The private elevator shafts, the rooftop terrace structures, and the ground-level pool garden footprints are all clearly legible in the framing. The project is on schedule for a Q2 2027 completion.

title: Haven — November 2025
excerpt: This month we take a closer look at the two finish collections — Brisa and Noir — and explore the design philosophies that shaped them.

body.text: Two Visions, One Standard

Casa Avenida offers two distinct finish packages — Brisa and Noir — because we believe that luxury should reflect the individual, not a single predetermined aesthetic. Both packages were developed by interior designer Stef Leonel and curated by Ava Gray, and both meet the same uncompromising standard of quality. The difference is philosophical.
Brisa: The Coastal Collection

Brisa takes its cues from the South Florida landscape — from sea breeze and warm afternoon light, from the natural palette of sand, stone, and bleached oak. Natural oak wide-plank flooring runs throughout the living areas. Cabinetry is flat-panel in natural oak veneer with integrated pulls. Countertops are Calacatta cream marble with warm gold veining. The effect is luminous by day and intimate by evening — a home that breathes.
Noir: The Dark Dramatic Collection

Noir is an exercise in considered restraint — the luxury of darkness, of depth, of rooms that demand your presence. Charcoal stained hardwood floors. Flat-panel cabinetry in deep espresso lacquer. Nero Marquina marble with white veining. Matte black fixtures. Where Brisa opens, Noir envelops. Both are extraordinary. The choice is entirely yours.

title: Haven — January 2026
excerpt: The new year brings fresh energy to the Casa Avenida site. We share construction milestones, market insights, and a preview of what life at 102 SE 5th Ave will feel like.

body.text: A New Year at 102 SE 5th Ave

Construction continues at pace. The framing is complete on all eight residences, and attention has turned to the building envelope — windows, glazing, and the rooftop terrace structures that will define the skyline presence of Casa Avenida from Atlantic Avenue.

The private elevator shafts are enclosed, the pool garden excavations are finished, and the mechanical, electrical, and plumbing rough-in is now underway. Interior fit-out — the moment when Brisa and Noir come to life — is targeted for Q3 2026.
The Market in 2026

Delray Beach enters the new year with its strongest fundamentals in recent memory. Inventory in the boutique luxury segment remains critically low. Demand from Northeast and Midwest buyers — professionals, families, and retirees alike — shows no sign of abating. For buyers considering Casa Avenida, the window to secure a preferred residence at pre-completion pricing is narrowing.

We encourage serious buyers to contact our sales team at Douglas Elliman to schedule a private briefing and review available residences. TJ Verdiglione and Nicole Melveney are available by appointment.
